en.title: Best Handheld Gaming Consoles 2026
en.description: The Switch 2, ROG Xbox Ally X, and Steam Deck OLED finally arrived in the same year — here is how every serious 2026 handheld actually stacks up.

zh-tw.title: 最佳手持遊戲機 2026
zh-tw.description: Switch 2、ROG Xbox Ally X 和 Steam Deck OLED 終於在同一年到齊，這份排行幫你把 2026 年所有值得買的掌機重新排好。

en.description: Valve's 7.4-inch HDR OLED handheld at 90Hz running SteamOS 3. After May's hike the 1TB model now lists at $949.99, the priciest route into SteamOS, and it stays the most polished pick-up-and-play PC handheld in the category.

zh-tw.description: Valve 推出的 7.4 吋 HDR OLED 90Hz 掌機，跑 SteamOS 3。五月漲價後 1TB 版本定價來到 949 美元，台灣水貨大約落在三萬上下，是進入 SteamOS 最貴的方式，但它依然是最順手的 PC 掌機。

en.description: Nintendo's hybrid sequel with a 7.9-inch 1080p HDR LCD at 120Hz, 4K dock output, and the only handheld that runs Mario Kart World, Donkey Kong Bananza, and the Zelda re-releases.

zh-tw.description: 任天堂的二代混合主機，7.9 吋 1080p HDR LCD 120Hz 螢幕，接基座輸出 4K，唯一玩得到第一方 Switch 2 大作的機器。

en.description: Ryzen AI Z2 Extreme, 24GB RAM, 1TB SSD, 80Wh battery, and an Xbox-skinned Windows 11 shell that finally hides the desktop on a $999.99 flagship.

zh-tw.description: Ryzen AI Z2 Extreme、24GB RAM、1TB SSD、80Wh 大電池，Xbox 主導的 Windows 11 介面終於把桌面藏起來，是 2026 年最強的 Windows 掌機。

en.description: 8.8-inch 144Hz OLED, Ryzen Z2 Extreme, up to 32GB RAM, detachable controllers and a built-in kickstand — the most ambitious Windows handheld of 2026.

zh-tw.description: 8.8 吋 144Hz OLED 螢幕、Ryzen Z2 Extreme、最高 32GB RAM、可拆式手把加內建支架，2026 年規格最浮誇的 Windows 掌機。

en.description: 8-inch 1200p 120Hz VRR display with Ryzen Z2 Extreme, 32GB RAM, and the largest 80Wh battery in its class — the cooler-running Windows alternative to the Ally X.

zh-tw.description: 8 吋 1200p 120Hz VRR 螢幕，搭 Ryzen Z2 Extreme、32GB RAM 和同級最大的 80Wh 電池，是 Ally X 之外溫度控制更好的 Windows 對手。

en.description: First non-Valve handheld officially shipping with SteamOS — Z1 Extreme, 32GB RAM, 8-inch 120Hz VRR LCD, and a real shot at Steam Deck buyers shut out by stock shortages.

zh-tw.description: 第一台非 Valve 官方預載 SteamOS 的掌機，Z1 Extreme、32GB RAM、8 吋 120Hz VRR LCD，買不到 Steam Deck 時的最佳替代品。

en.description: 7-inch 144Hz OLED with modular swappable control faceplates, Ryzen AI 9 HX 370, OCuLink for external GPUs — the enthusiast handheld that treats hardware as a hobby.

zh-tw.description: 7 吋 144Hz OLED 螢幕、模組化可換手把面板、Ryzen AI 9 HX 370、支援 OCuLink 外接顯卡，是給把硬體當興趣的發燒友玩的掌機。

en.description: Slide-up physical QWERTY keyboard, Ryzen AI 9 HX 370, 6-inch 144Hz screen — the only mainstream handheld in 2026 that lets you actually type without docking.

zh-tw.description: 螢幕上推就有實體 QWERTY 鍵盤，搭 Ryzen AI 9 HX 370、6 吋 144Hz 螢幕，是 2026 年少數不用接基座就能正常打字的掌機。

en.description: Strix Halo Ryzen AI Max+ 395 flagship with a 9.06-inch OLED, the fastest handheld silicon made and priced like a gaming PC.

zh-tw.description: 搭 Strix Halo Ryzen AI Max+ 395 的旗艦，9.06 吋 OLED，目前最快的掌機晶片，價格也開得像一台電競主機。

Steam Deck OLED in 2026 Review: Performance, Value, and Buying Advice

en.commentary:
2026 is the first year the handheld market makes sense as a single comparison instead of three siloed conversations. Switch 2 is finally out, the ROG Xbox Ally X has shipped, the Legion Go 2 and Claw 8 AI+ ended the year-long preview cycle, and Steam Deck OLED is still the king of the SteamOS ecosystem. I ranked these the way an actual buyer would rank them — by what you do on day one, not by spec sheets.

Steam Deck OLED takes first place because it is the only handheld in this list that costs $649 and asks nothing else from you. Boot it up, sign in, and your existing Steam library is downloading. SteamOS is faster, more polished, and more battery-efficient than Windows on every Windows handheld in this comparison, and Valve's hardware team kept the weight and grip geometry right. The 7.4-inch HDR OLED at 90Hz is still the best display-to-price ratio in the category. The only real warning is stock — Valve has been chronically out of the 1TB SKU through Q1 2026, and refurbished units are now the most reliable way to buy.

Switch 2 is second on a different axis entirely. You do not buy it to compete with PC handhelds on performance. You buy it because Mario Kart World, the new Donkey Kong, and the entire first-party Switch 2 catalog only exist on this hardware. The 7.9-inch HDR LCD at 120Hz, 4K dock output, and a $449.99 price tag make it the most accessible handheld in the comparison and the only one that families and casual players should consider first. If you only buy one gaming handheld in 2026 and you are not already on PC, this is it.

The ROG Xbox Ally X is third and represents the largest single-year improvement of any handheld in this list. The 80Wh battery, 24GB of RAM, 1TB SSD, and the new Xbox full-screen experience that finally hides the Windows desktop make this the first Windows handheld I would recommend without an asterisk. At $999.99, it is twice the Switch 2 and you should know exactly why you are paying for that — Game Pass on the go, full Steam, full Epic, mod support, and the headroom to actually run 2026 AAA games at native resolution.

Legion Go 2 at four wins on raw spec ambition. The 8.8-inch 144Hz OLED is the best display on any handheld and the detachable controllers plus kickstand are genuinely useful for travel and couch play. It is heavier and more expensive than the Ally X, and the Lenovo software shell is still rougher than what Asus and Microsoft built together, which is why it sits one rank lower despite the better screen.

MSI Claw 8 AI+ is fifth and quietly excellent. Its Intel-based predecessors were thermally compromised, but the Ryzen Z2 Extreme refresh closed the gap and the 80Wh battery matches the Ally X. It loses points only because the Ally X has the platform-level Xbox integration that MSI cannot replicate.

Legion Go S with SteamOS at six is the most interesting story in the category — Valve licensed SteamOS to a third party, and the result is a real Steam Deck alternative for buyers who cannot find one in stock. The Z1 Extreme is roughly Steam Deck OLED level in real-world games, and the 8-inch 120Hz screen is bigger if not as bright as Valve's OLED.

AYANEO 3 and GPD Win 4 close out the list at seven and eight, and they are there for the enthusiasts who already know they want them. Modular face controllers, OCuLink for external GPUs, sliding QWERTY keyboards — these are wonderful niche features and they justify the price for the right buyer. For anyone else, the top six exist for a reason.

highlights.title: Steam Deck OLED is still the only handheld where the software is the feature, not the obstacle
highlights.body: Every Windows handheld in this list is fighting against Windows. SteamOS is the inverse — it boots straight into a console-style game launcher, manages cloud saves and proton compatibility automatically, sleeps and resumes flawlessly, and ships with controller-native menus everywhere. The Z2 Extreme handhelds beat the Steam Deck OLED on raw FPS, but the Steam Deck wins on actually getting to those FPS without 20 minutes of driver and overlay troubleshooting. At $649 for the 1TB OLED model, it remains the highest-confidence purchase in the category.

highlights.title: The Switch 2 is the only $449 console in the world that runs Mario Kart World
highlights.body: Hardware comparisons miss the point on this one. Switch 2 sits in a parallel market where the question is not 'how does it benchmark against the Ally X' but 'do you want to play the new 3D Mario, the new Zelda, the new Donkey Kong, and the new Pokemon on a portable.' First-party Nintendo games stay exclusive and the price is half of what a comparable Windows handheld costs. For the family-buyer market, the casual-buyer market, and the lapsed-gamer market, this is the entire conversation.

highlights.title: The ROG Xbox Ally X is the first Windows handheld I trust without a but
highlights.body: Two things changed in 2026. The 80Wh battery turned the Ally line from a four-hour handheld into a six-hour handheld. And the Xbox full-screen experience finally boots straight into a Series-S-style game launcher that hides the Windows desktop entirely. Combined with 24GB of RAM, a 1TB SSD, and impulse triggers, this is the first time a Windows handheld has felt like a console product instead of a desktop in disguise. At $999.99 it is expensive, but it is the only handheld in the list that runs Steam, Game Pass, Epic, and 2026 AAA games on the same device with no compromises.

highlights.title: Legion Go 2 has the best screen, MSI Claw 8 AI+ has the best thermals, and both lose narrowly to the Ally X
highlights.body: Lenovo's 8.8-inch 144Hz OLED is genuinely the prettiest panel in the category and the detachable controllers solve a real problem for desk and couch use. MSI's 80Wh battery and quieter cooling are measurable wins. But neither manages the platform-level integration that Microsoft and Asus built into the Ally X, and at this price point the software experience is what separates a $1,000 device that you actually use from one that ends up in a drawer.

highlights.title: Legion Go S with SteamOS is the answer to 'I cannot find a Steam Deck OLED in stock'
highlights.body: Valve licensing SteamOS to Lenovo is the single most consequential platform move of 2026 for handheld gaming. Z1 Extreme matches Steam Deck OLED performance closely in real games, the 8-inch 120Hz LCD is bigger than Valve's screen, and crucially, the Legion Go S is actually available to buy. Buyers who tried for months to grab a 1TB Steam Deck OLED and gave up should put this on their shortlist immediately.

zh-tw.commentary:
2026 年是手持遊戲機市場第一次可以放在同一張桌子上比較的一年。Switch 2 終於上市、ROG Xbox Ally X 出貨、Legion Go 2 和 Claw 8 AI+ 結束一整年的預告期，Steam Deck OLED 也還是 SteamOS 陣營的王者。這份排行我是用一般人實際買回家會怎麼用的角度來排，而不是看規格表。

Steam Deck OLED 排第一，因為這份名單裡只有它買回家不需要再跟系統打架。一台 21,000 元出頭，開機登入，你的 Steam 遊戲庫就在下載。SteamOS 比這次比較裡所有 Windows 掌機都跑得更順、更省電，Valve 硬體團隊把重量和握把角度也抓得剛好，7.4 吋 HDR OLED 90Hz 螢幕在這個價位還是同級最強。唯一要注意的是缺貨，1TB 版本在 2026 年第一季幾乎一直缺，目前比較穩的買法反而是去買 Valve 官網翻新機。

Switch 2 排第二是另一個維度的勝出。買這台不是為了跟 PC 掌機比效能，是因為 Mario Kart World、新 Donkey Kong、整個 Switch 2 第一方陣容只有這台機器玩得到。7.9 吋 HDR LCD 120Hz、接基座輸出 4K、台幣不到 14,000 的入手價，是這份名單裡門檻最低的一台。如果你 2026 年只想買一台掌機而且本來就不是 PC 玩家，那答案就是它。

ROG Xbox Ally X 排第三，是這份名單裡單年進步幅度最大的一台。80Wh 電池、24GB RAM、1TB SSD、新的 Xbox 全螢幕體驗終於把 Windows 桌面藏起來，這是我第一次推薦 Windows 掌機可以不加但書。台幣三萬多是 Switch 2 的兩倍多，你買它的理由要很清楚，Game Pass 隨身玩、Steam、Epic、Mod 支援、跑 2026 年 AAA 大作都沒問題。

Legion Go 2 排第四，規格野心最大。8.8 吋 144Hz OLED 是同級最強的螢幕，可拆式手把加內建支架對出差和沙發玩確實實用。但比 Ally X 重、貴，Lenovo 的軟體層做得也比微軟跟華碩聯手做出來的粗糙，所以螢幕雖然贏，整體還是排在後一名。

MSI Claw 8 AI+ 排第五，是低調但表現很好的一台。前一代 Intel 版本散熱有問題，但這次換 Ryzen Z2 Extreme 之後追上來了，80Wh 電池跟 Ally X 一樣大。扣分的地方在於 Ally X 有 Xbox 平台層級的整合，MSI 做不出來這層體驗。

Legion Go S 跑 SteamOS 排第六，是這個品類最有趣的故事。Valve 把 SteamOS 授權給第三方，買不到 Steam Deck 的人現在有真正的替代品。Z1 Extreme 在實際遊戲表現大概等於 Steam Deck OLED，8 吋 120Hz 螢幕比 Valve 那塊大，雖然亮度不到 OLED 那麼漂亮。

AYANEO 3 和 GPD Win 4 排七、八，是給已經知道自己要什麼的發燒友玩的。模組化手把面板、OCuLink 外接顯卡、滑蓋實體鍵盤，這些都是非常棒的小眾功能，但如果你不知道自己需不需要，那前六名才是你該看的選項。

highlights.title: Steam Deck OLED 還是唯一一台軟體是賣點而不是阻礙的掌機
highlights.body: 這份名單裡每一台 Windows 掌機都在跟 Windows 對抗。SteamOS 反過來，開機直接進主機風格的遊戲啟動器，雲端存檔、Proton 相容性自動處理，睡眠喚醒順暢無痛，所有選單都是手把優先設計。Z2 Extreme 的掌機在純 FPS 數字上贏過 Steam Deck OLED，但 Steam Deck 贏在實際開機 20 分鐘內你就在玩遊戲，而不是還在處理驅動和疊加層。一台 1TB OLED 版本台幣 21,000 元，是這個品類最有把握的一筆消費。

highlights.title: Switch 2 是全世界唯一一台 14,000 元就能玩 Mario Kart World 的主機
highlights.body: 比規格在這台機器上沒意義。Switch 2 站在平行宇宙，問題不是「跟 Ally X 跑分差多少」，是「想不想在掌機上玩新 3D Mario、新 Zelda、新 Donkey Kong、新寶可夢」。任天堂第一方遊戲就是只在這台機器上獨佔，價格只要同級 Windows 掌機的一半。家庭買家、休閒玩家、回鍋玩家，這台就是答案，沒有第二個選項要討論。

highlights.title: ROG Xbox Ally X 是我第一次推薦不用加但書的 Windows 掌機
highlights.body: 2026 年有兩件事改變了這條產品線。80Wh 電池把 Ally 從四小時掌機升級到六小時掌機。Xbox 全螢幕體驗終於開機直接進 Series S 風格的啟動器，把 Windows 桌面完全藏起來。再加上 24GB RAM、1TB SSD、震動扳機，這是第一次有 Windows 掌機讓我覺得它真的是主機產品，不是偽裝成主機的桌機。台幣三萬多是貴，但它是這份名單裡唯一一台可以同時跑 Steam、Game Pass、Epic 和 2026 年 AAA 大作完全沒妥協的機器。

highlights.title: Legion Go 2 螢幕最強、MSI Claw 8 AI+ 散熱最好，但兩台都微幅輸給 Ally X
highlights.body: Lenovo 那塊 8.8 吋 144Hz OLED 是同級最漂亮的螢幕，可拆式手把對桌上型和沙發玩確實有解決問題。MSI 的 80Wh 電池和更安靜的散熱也是可量測的優勢。但這兩家都沒辦法做到微軟和華碩聯手在 Ally X 上做到的平台層整合，在這個價位帶，軟體體驗才是決定一台台幣三萬多的掌機你會天天用還是丟在抽屜的關鍵。

highlights.title: Legion Go S 的 SteamOS 版，是「我買不到 Steam Deck OLED」的標準答案
highlights.body: Valve 把 SteamOS 授權給 Lenovo 是 2026 年掌機平台最重要的單一事件。Z1 Extreme 在實際遊戲表現上跟 Steam Deck OLED 接近，8 吋 120Hz 螢幕比 Valve 那塊大，最重要的是 Legion Go S 真的買得到。已經試了好幾個月想搶 1TB Steam Deck OLED 結果搶不到的人，這台應該直接放進清單。

Steam Deck OLED in 2026 Review: Performance, Value, and Buying Advice

en.commentary: Handheld gaming consoles are the most contested category of 2026 and the rankings have a clear story. Steam Deck OLED holds first because Valve continues to ship the best value-per-dollar handheld and SteamOS has matured into the most polished handheld OS. The OLED screen, the trackpads for non-controller games, and the modding community give it a depth no Windows handheld matches. Nintendo Switch 2 at #2 is the first-party games argument and at $450 it is the only handheld that plays Mario Kart World, Donkey Kong Bananza, and the upcoming Metroid Prime 4. ROG Xbox Ally X at #3 is the Windows handheld that finally got the OS right — Microsoft's Xbox handheld mode strips Windows down to a real handheld interface. Lenovo Legion Go 2 at #4 is the detachable-controller niche pick. MSI Claw 8 AI+ at #5 is the Intel handheld for buyers who want Intel for some reason. Lenovo Legion Go S SteamOS at #6 is interesting because it brings SteamOS to a non-Valve handheld. AYANEO 3 at #7 and GPD Win 4 (2025) at #8 are the boutique enthusiast picks. Pick Steam Deck OLED for everything except first-party Nintendo games.

highlights.title: Steam Deck OLED holds first because SteamOS is the only handheld OS that respects the form factor
highlights.body: Windows on a handheld still requires the on-screen keyboard for half the menus. SteamOS on the Deck OLED is built for the controller and the screen size from the ground up. The trackpads make non-controller games playable. At $549 for the 512GB model it is half the price of the ROG Xbox Ally X and the better handheld.

highlights.title: Nintendo Switch 2 is the first-party games argument and that argument wins
highlights.body: Mario Kart World, Donkey Kong Bananza, and the upcoming Metroid Prime 4 will only ever run on a Switch. At $450 the Switch 2 is the second handheld I tell people to buy after the Steam Deck OLED, and the only handheld that plays the games Nintendo refuses to port.

highlights.title: ROG Xbox Ally X is the Windows handheld that finally got the OS right
highlights.body: Microsoft's Xbox handheld mode strips Windows down to a real handheld interface. The AMD Ryzen Z2 Extreme is the most powerful chip in any handheld. At $1,000 it is twice the Steam Deck OLED price and only worth it if you specifically need Windows-only games or hardware acceleration the Steam Deck cannot do.

highlights.title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 is the right detachable-controller pick if that matters
highlights.body: The detachable controllers fix the only Switch 2 weakness — the joycon drift. Lenovo's controller engineering is a generation ahead. At $899 it is priced against the ROG Ally X and the choice is whether you want detachable controllers or raw performance.

highlights.title: AYANEO 3 and GPD Win 4 (2025) are boutique picks for enthusiasts only
highlights.body: Both are excellent handhelds with Windows that work, but at $1,200-plus they cost twice the Steam Deck OLED for marginal performance gains. Buy them if you specifically want a clamshell or a slider form factor. Otherwise, the Steam Deck OLED is the better value at every angle.

zh-tw.commentary: 手持遊戲機是 2026 年競爭最激烈的品類，排行有很清楚的故事線。Steam Deck OLED 守住第一是因為 Valve 持續推出最高 CP 值機型，SteamOS 成熟成最完整的手持作業系統。OLED 螢幕、給非控制器遊戲用的觸控板、改機社群深度，沒有任何 Windows 掌機能比。Nintendo Switch 2 排第二是第一方遊戲論述，台灣 13,500 元是唯一能玩 Mario Kart World、Donkey Kong Bananza、即將推出的 Metroid Prime 4 的掌機。ROG Xbox Ally X 排第三是終於把作業系統做對的 Windows 掌機，微軟 Xbox 手持模式把 Windows 砍成真正掌機介面。Lenovo Legion Go 2 排第四是可拆控制器的小眾選擇。MSI Claw 8 AI+ 排第五是想要 Intel 的買家用的。Lenovo Legion Go S SteamOS 排第六很有意思，把 SteamOS 帶到非 Valve 掌機。AYANEO 3 排第七、GPD Win 4 (2025) 排第八是發燒友精品選擇。除了任天堂第一方遊戲外，全部選 Steam Deck OLED。

highlights.title: Steam Deck OLED 守住第一是因為 SteamOS 是唯一尊重手持形式的作業系統
highlights.body: Windows 在掌機上一半的選單還是要叫出螢幕鍵盤。SteamOS 在 Deck OLED 從頭到尾為控制器跟螢幕尺寸設計。觸控板讓非控制器遊戲可以玩。512GB 版台灣 17,000 元是 ROG Xbox Ally X 一半的價格，而且是更好的掌機。

highlights.title: Nintendo Switch 2 是第一方遊戲論述，這個論述贏了
highlights.body: Mario Kart World、Donkey Kong Bananza、即將推出的 Metroid Prime 4 永遠只在 Switch 上跑。台灣 13,500 元是 Steam Deck OLED 之後我推薦的第二台掌機，也是唯一能玩任天堂不肯移植的遊戲的選擇。

highlights.title: ROG Xbox Ally X 是終於把作業系統做對的 Windows 掌機
highlights.body: 微軟 Xbox 手持模式把 Windows 砍成真正掌機介面。AMD Ryzen Z2 Extreme 是任何掌機最強的晶片。台灣 33,000 元是 Steam Deck OLED 兩倍價，只有特別需要 Windows 限定遊戲或 Steam Deck 跑不動的硬體加速才值得。

highlights.title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 是可拆控制器派的正解
highlights.body: 可拆控制器解決 Switch 2 唯一弱點，就是 Joy-Con 漂移。Lenovo 的控制器工程領先一個世代。台灣 30,000 元定價打 ROG Ally X，要可拆控制器還是要原始效能而已。

highlights.title: AYANEO 3 跟 GPD Win 4 (2025) 是發燒友精品選擇
highlights.body: 兩台都是 Windows 跑得動的優秀掌機，但台灣 40,000 元以上是 Steam Deck OLED 兩倍價，效能進步有限。要折疊式或滑蓋式形式才買它們。否則 Steam Deck OLED 在每個角度都是更好的性價比。

en.commentary: I've spent the better part of April rotating between five handhelds on planes, couches, and a hotel room in Taipei, and the verdict landed firmer than I expected. Steam Deck OLED 1TB stays at the top because Valve's SteamOS 3.7 update genuinely smoothed out the Proton compatibility list, and the 90Hz HDR OLED panel still embarrasses Windows handhelds twice its price. Switch 2 sits second on the strength of exclusives alone. Mario Kart World and the new Metroid have been my flight-mode go-tos, and the 7.9-inch 1080p 120Hz LCD is finally a screen I don't apologize for. The ROG Xbox Ally X 2025 is the Windows handheld I actually keep in my bag, since the Xbox shell on top of Windows 11 fixed the suspend-resume nightmare that ruined earlier Allys for me. Lenovo's Legion Go 2 Gen 2 is in fourth place today, but the SteamOS variant launching in June 2026 is going to shake this list hard, an 8.8-inch 1920x1200 OLED at 144Hz running native SteamOS is the spec sheet most of us have wanted since 2023. MSI's Claw 8 AI+ A8 closes the top five with the best build quality of any Windows handheld but a higher price than its software experience justifies. The honest reality of May 2026: Valve owns ergonomics and software, Nintendo owns the games, and Windows handhelds are still figuring out what they want to be when they grow up.

highlights.title: Steam Deck OLED is still the default recommendation
highlights.body: I keep waiting for a Windows handheld to dethrone Valve, and every quarter I get more convinced that won't happen until Microsoft fixes Windows itself. SteamOS sleep, instant resume, controller mapping, and Proton's expanded library mean I can buy a game on Friday night and play it in five minutes flat. The 1TB OLED model is what you should buy, the 512GB LCD is yesterday's hardware at this point.

highlights.title: Switch 2 finally feels like a 2026 console in the hand
highlights.body: The bigger 1080p 120Hz LCD, magnetic Joy-Cons that don't drift, and meaningful CPU horsepower mean third-party ports actually run well now. I played the new Final Fantasy XVI handheld port on a flight to Tokyo and forgot I wasn't on a Steam Deck. If Nintendo keeps the first-party cadence up through this fall, the Switch 2 will hold its number two spot easily.

highlights.title: Legion Go 2 SteamOS in June 2026 is the upgrade to wait for
highlights.body: Lenovo confirmed the SteamOS variant will ship with the same 8.8-inch 1920x1200 OLED 144Hz panel but skip the Windows licensing tax and the OEM crapware that ruined the original Legion Go for me. If the price comes in under $750 as the leaks suggest, this is the Steam Deck OLED successor Valve hasn't built yet, and it will jump to second place on this list the day reviews drop.

highlights.title: Steam Deck OLED 仍然是預設推薦
highlights.body: 我每一季都在等 Windows 掌機把 Valve 拉下來，每一季都更確定 Microsoft 不修好 Windows 之前這件事不會發生。SteamOS 的睡眠、即時喚醒、手把對應跟 Proton 擴大的支援名單，意思是我可以禮拜五晚上買遊戲，五分鐘後就能玩。1TB OLED 是現在要買的版本，512GB LCD 在這個價位已經算過時硬體。

highlights.title: Switch 2 拿在手上終於有 2026 年該有的樣子
highlights.body: 更大的 1080p 120Hz LCD、不會漂移的磁吸 Joy-Con，加上有意義的 CPU 效能升級，第三方移植版本現在跑起來真的順。我在飛東京的航班上玩新的 FF16 掌機版，差點忘記自己不是在用 Steam Deck。如果任天堂下半年第一方節奏維持住，Switch 2 第二名穩穩的。

highlights.title: 六月的 Legion Go 2 SteamOS 是值得等的升級
highlights.body: 聯想已經確認 SteamOS 版本會用一樣的 8.8 吋 1920x1200 OLED 144Hz 面板，但省掉 Windows 授權費跟 OEM 預裝廢軟體，這些東西當初害我退掉初代 Legion Go。如果定價真的像爆料說的壓在 NT$23,000 以內，這台就是 Valve 自己還沒做出來的 Steam Deck OLED 後繼機，評測一出我會直接把它換到這份榜單第二名。

highlights.title: Steam Deck OLED is still the answer for most buyers in May 2026

en.commentary: Bad news for handheld buyers this week. Component price increases are starting to push handheld pricing in directions nobody wanted. The Legion Go S jumping from $900 to $1,579 is the canary in the coal mine, and the Legion Go 2 launching in June at $1,199 confirms the trend. Lenovo Legion Go S drops a tier on value because that price hike is brutal regardless of how good the platform is. The Steam Deck OLED stays on top because Valve's pricing discipline matters more every week. At $549 to $649 depending on storage, it's the only handheld with stable pricing and a mature SteamOS experience. The Nintendo Switch 2 at $450 is the second value champion and the only place to play Nintendo first-party titles, which is a non-negotiable for many buyers. The ROG Xbox Ally X is the performance pick if you have the budget and want Windows handheld flexibility, the Z2 Extreme really does run AAA games at meaningful settings. The Legion Go 2 will overlap with it but at $1,199 it's hard to recommend over the Ally X. The MSI Claw 8 AI+ is the workmanlike Windows alternative, fine but not exciting. Ayaneo 3 stays niche, the build quality is genuinely improved but the value math hurts when Steam Deck OLED exists. The GPD Win 4 2025 is for clamshell loyalists, accept the ergonomics tradeoff for the form factor.

highlights.title: Pricing pressure hits the segment
highlights.body: Legion Go S jumped from $900 to $1,579 and Legion Go 2 launches at $1,199. Component costs are pushing the entire Windows handheld category into territory most buyers won't accept.

highlights.title: Steam Deck OLED is the price stability winner
highlights.body: Valve's $549 to $649 pricing has held while everyone else moves up. SteamOS maturity makes this the safe buy.

highlights.title: Switch 2 stays the family pick
highlights.body: $450 plus exclusive Nintendo titles makes this the only handheld for households where game library matters more than raw power.

highlights.title: Xbox Ally X earns the performance crown
highlights.body: Z2 Extreme delivers real AAA performance for buyers who need Windows. The Ally X is the one to buy if you can swallow the price.

en.commentary: First Tuesday after Mother's Day weekend, and the handheld picture is steady because the Computex pre-show announcements that could shake this list are still days away. Steam Deck OLED is the recommendation I make ninety percent of the time. SteamOS is the most polished handheld OS, the HDR OLED panel still beats every Windows-handheld screen in the same size class, and the price is the right anchor in the category. Nintendo Switch 2 sits at two because the exclusive library is what makes a handheld worth owning for households, and the joycon redesign finally addresses drift. ROG Xbox Ally X is the Windows handheld I tell power users to buy because the Xbox-mode UI hides Windows competently. Legion Go 2 holds four for the detachable controllers and the 144Hz OLED, but the battery still loses against Ally X. AYANEO 3 and GPD Win 4 2025 finish the list as enthusiast picks. No movement, and that is the honest read. For households deciding between handhelds this week, I keep telling people the same thing: pick the platform that matches your library before you pick the spec sheet. Hardware shifts every nine months, but a Steam library or a Nintendo library lasts a decade.

Nintendo Switch 2 holds second and the exclusive software calendar through H2 keeps building, which means the gap to first will narrow on raw mindshare even though the Deck wins on hardware versatility. ROG Xbox Ally X holds third and the major update this week that finally makes the dock-mode display switching behave correctly is a real fix for the most consistent complaint from owners. The sales stall is a separate problem and one that pricing alone is not going to solve, because the software story is still the friction point. Lenovo Legion Go 2 Gen 2 stays at fourth. The Windows handheld category is overdue for consolidation and the next twelve months will decide which OEMs survive.

en.commentary: Friday morning the handheld gaming category opened with Best Buy's Memorial Day gaming sale running over 1,300 deals and the lineup mostly held position. ROG Xbox Ally X (2025) at third drops to $799 with the Best Buy MD cut of $100, the new AMD Z2 Extreme plus the Xbox app integration plus the cloud handoff to Series X is the right pick for buyers locked into the Xbox ecosystem, and the price now actually competes with the Steam Deck for high-end PC gaming on the go. Lenovo Legion Go 2 holds fourth at $749 with the Lenovo direct $100 cut, the 8.8-inch QHD display plus the detachable controllers is the right pick for buyers who want versatility but the battery life still trails the Steam Deck. MSI Claw 8 AI+ holds fifth at $899, the Intel Core Ultra 7 plus the larger 80Wh battery is the right pick for Intel buyers who care about battery life over peak performance. Verdict for Friday: Steam Deck OLED at $549 is the buy of the weekend for the open PC library, Switch 2 at $449 if you want first-party Nintendo, ROG Xbox Ally X at $799 if you live in Xbox Game Pass. I have been running the Steam Deck OLED, the Switch 2, and the ROG Xbox Ally X in rotation since January, and after a full holiday weekend of testing on the couch, on the patio, and on a four-hour flight back from a wedding in Denver, my ranking has actually settled. Valve's Steam Deck OLED at $649 remains the one I reach for first. The 90Hz HDR panel still looks fantastic with summer blockbuster games like Hades II and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, and SteamOS 3 boots straight into your library with zero Windows nonsense. For families and Nintendo loyalists, the Switch 2 at $449.99 is the easiest recommendation I give all year. Mario Kart World and Donkey Kong Bananza are the kind of exclusives that justify owning a second handheld even if you already have a PC option. The ROG Xbox Ally X at $999.99 is the one I bring on trips when I want to play my Xbox Game Pass library natively, and the new Xbox shell on Windows 11 finally feels designed for a controller. Lenovo's Legion Go 2 Gen 2 at $1,449.99 is the spec king with its 8.8-inch 144Hz OLED, while the MSI Claw 8 AI+ matches the Ally X on silicon with cooler thermals. Today is the day to pull the trigger; the bundle pricing I am seeing at Best Buy and Newegg will not survive Tuesday.

en.commentary: The Steam Deck OLED holds the top position in handheld gaming because it delivers the broadest combination of library access, display quality, and value that no competitor currently matches as a complete package. Its 9.5 scores in library, display, ergonomics, and value are not accidental: Valve's SteamOS ecosystem and the OLED panel's contrast and battery optimization make it the default recommendation for PC gaming on the go. The Nintendo Switch 2 at rank two is the answer for buyers who want Nintendo-exclusive titles and a family-friendly ecosystem. Its $449.99 price, 9-hour battery life, and 9.5 library score reflect the fact that Mario Kart World and the Switch 2 exclusive catalog cannot be replicated anywhere else. The ROG Ally X with Xbox branding at rank three carries a 9.5 performance score from its AMD Ryzen Z2 Extreme and 24GB RAM configuration, making it the strongest raw-performance handheld available. At $799, it is a deliberate purchase for buyers who want AAA PC gaming without a desk. Father's Day is pushing all three top handhelds into heavy gift guide rotation this week. The Lenovo Legion Go 2 at rank four combines a 9.5 display score with its large format screen and detachable controllers, while the MSI Claw 8 AI+ rounds out the top five with a balanced feature set.

en.commentary: Pricing turbulence is reshaping this category, and it is why I am holding my order steady rather than reacting. Valve raised the Steam Deck OLED price by 240 dollars in May, citing higher memory and storage costs, and Nintendo has confirmed a 50 dollar bump on the Switch 2 coming in September. The wider RAMageddon, the AI-driven spike in memory prices, is hitting every maker at once. Even with the increase, the Steam Deck OLED keeps my top spot. Its 7.4-inch HDR OLED at up to 90Hz still produces the bright color and deep blacks that define the handheld experience, and SteamOS remains the most polished portable platform with the deepest verified library. The Switch 2 holds second and is simply the best way to play Nintendo games in 2026, with its 7.9-inch 1080p 120Hz LCD, 256GB of storage, and 4K docked output via a custom Nvidia chip. If you can buy before September, do it and save the 50 dollars. The ROG Xbox Ally X stays third as the performance pick. Its Ryzen Z2 Extreme, 24GB of RAM, and fast 1TB SSD chew through demanding games that bring the Switch 2 and Steam Deck to their knees, and it wins by default if you want serious horsepower without a near-1,000-dollar Legion Go 2. Below that, the Legion Go 2 and MSI Claw 8 AI+ hold their spots for spec chasers, and the SteamOS Legion Go S remains a strong value entry. My advice this week is timing-driven. If you want a handheld, the smart move is to buy before the next round of price hikes lands.

zh-tw.commentary: 價格亂流正在重塑這個品類，這也是我這週選擇維持排名、而不是急著反應的原因。Valve 五月把 Steam Deck OLED 漲了 240 美元，理由是記憶體跟儲存成本上升，而 Nintendo 也確認 Switch 2 九月要漲 50 美元。背後是更大範圍的 RAMageddon，也就是 AI 帶動的記憶體價格暴漲，這波每家廠商一起中招。就算漲了，Steam Deck OLED 還是守住我的第一。它那塊 7.4 吋、最高 90Hz 的 HDR OLED 依然能給出定義掌機體驗的鮮豔發色跟深邃黑位，SteamOS 仍是最成熟的掌機平台，認證遊戲庫也最深。Switch 2 守第二，2026 年要玩任天堂遊戲它就是最佳途徑，7.9 吋 1080p 120Hz LCD、256GB 儲存、靠客製 Nvidia 晶片還能 4K 接電視輸出。能在九月前入手就趕快，省那 50 美元。ROG Xbox Ally X 守第三，是效能首選。Ryzen Z2 Extreme、24GB RAM 加上快速 1TB SSD，那些把 Switch 2 跟 Steam Deck 逼到極限的大作它都吃得下，想要真正馬力又不想花近千美元上 Legion Go 2，它幾乎是預設答案。再往下，Legion Go 2 跟 MSI Claw 8 AI+ 守住規格控的位置，SteamOS 版 Legion Go S 仍是很強的性價比入門款。這週的建議跟時機綁在一起，想買掌機，聰明的做法就是趕在下一波漲價落地前先卡位。台灣水貨價也會跟著國際行情走，動作要快。

references.title: Valve hikes Steam Deck OLED prices — 512GB is now $789, while 1TB climbs to $949
Valve jacks up Steam Deck prices by as much as $300
Best gaming handhelds 2026 ranked: Steam Deck, Xbox Ally X, Switch 2, Legion Go 2, MSI Claw 8 AI+, and more

en.commentary:
A genuine market event reshuffles the top of this list. On May 27, Valve raised Steam Deck OLED prices significantly, the 512GB model jumping from $549 to $789 and the 1TB from $649 to $949, citing the RAM and NAND flash shortage driven by AI data-center demand. The Steam Deck OLED held the top spot largely on an unbeatable value score, and at nearly 44% more expensive that argument no longer holds. It is still a superb handheld with its 7.4-inch HDR OLED screen, vast verified library, and excellent ergonomics, so it drops only to rank 2 rather than falling out of contention.

The Nintendo Switch 2 takes over rank 1 as a result. Its pricing is unaffected by this swing, and it is the definitive way to play Nintendo games in 2026. The 7.9-inch LCD supports HDR and runs up to 120fps, and docked it outputs 4K to compatible TVs. With the value calculus shifting, the Switch 2's combination of a stable price, a first-party library nothing else can match, and a polished hardware experience makes it the new front-runner.

The ASUS ROG Xbox Ally X holds rank 3 as the most powerful handheld PC option, with its Ryzen Z2 Extreme chip, 24GB of RAM, and up to 35W when plugged in, though its $1,000 price keeps it a premium pick. The Lenovo Legion Go 2 at rank 4 and MSI Claw 8 AI+ at rank 5 round out the high-performance Windows tier. The bigger story is that the RAM and NAND shortage is now hitting handheld pricing directly, and buyers should expect this pressure to persist through summer 2026. If you want a Steam Deck OLED, the value proposition has changed, and weighing it against the steady-priced Switch 2 is now the right move.

en.commentary: The Nintendo Switch 2 keeps my number one spot, and the value math makes it an easy call right now. It is a definitive upgrade over the original, a larger screen, 4K output when docked, and a pile of social and multiplayer features, and it remains the only way to play Nintendo's exclusives. For everyday gamers and families it is simply the most sensible handheld you can buy, which is why it sits on top. The Steam Deck OLED stays my pick for PC and indie gaming because that gorgeous OLED panel and open SteamOS library are still unmatched for sheer breadth. I want to flag the obvious caveat though: Valve raised prices sharply back in May, citing higher memory and storage component costs, so the Deck is a meaningfully bigger spend than it was a few months ago. That price move is exactly why the value gap to the Switch 2 has widened, and it is reflected in my scores. For maximum horsepower the ROG Xbox Ally X remains the one to beat, its Ryzen Z2 Extreme chip, 24GB of RAM, and higher power draw make it the clear choice for demanding PC games if you can stomach the cost. The Lenovo Legion Go 2, with its 8.8-inch 144Hz OLED, is the large-screen alternative arriving this month. My rankings hold this week, the hierarchy is settled: Switch 2 for value and exclusives, Steam Deck for the PC library, Ally X for raw power.

en.commentary: There is real movement at the top this week, and it comes straight from a price tag. Valve raised the Steam Deck OLED's price by 240 dollars in May 2026, blaming the surge in memory and storage costs that the industry is calling RAMageddon, and that hike dents the one thing the Steam Deck always led on: value. So I am sliding it from rank two to rank three and trimming its score to 8.9, with the ROG Xbox Ally X stepping up to rank two. To be clear, the Steam Deck OLED is still a brilliant handheld and SteamOS remains more efficient and easier to live with than Windows, with a deep well of community optimizations, so this is a conservative nudge driven by the new price, not a knock on the hardware. The Nintendo Switch 2 keeps number one because for most people it is simply the best way to play games on the go: a 7.9-inch 1080p 120Hz screen, 256GB of storage, a custom Nvidia chip with 4K docked output, and a first-party library nothing else can touch. The ROG Xbox Ally X earns its bump on raw Windows-handheld power, with a Ryzen Z2 Extreme chip, 24GB of RAM and a fast 1TB SSD. The Lenovo Legion Go 2 and MSI Claw 8 AI Plus hold their spots below. As of June 2026 the right handheld depends on whether you want the biggest library, the most power, or the lowest running cost.

en.commentary: June 2026 brings one piece of news that sharpens my handheld ranking without overturning it: Valve hiked the Steam Deck OLED price by 240 dollars in May, citing memory and storage costs, which pushes the 1TB model to 790 and up. That makes the Deck a tougher value proposition, and it is exactly why I keep the Nintendo Switch 2 at number one. The Switch 2 is the best way to play Nintendo's exclusives, runs a 7.9-inch 1080p 120Hz screen, outputs 4K 120fps docked for supported games, and at its price it is simply the easiest recommendation for families and everyday players. The ROG Xbox Ally X holds second and remains the performance king of the PC handhelds, with its Ryzen Z2 Extreme, 24GB of RAM and up to 35W plugged in. For raw Windows gaming power it more or less wins by default among handhelds that do not cost close to a thousand dollars. The Steam Deck OLED stays third, because even after the price jump that gorgeous HDR OLED panel, the unmatched SteamOS experience and the best ergonomics on the board keep it essential, though the value math is now closer. Lenovo's Legion Go 2 holds fourth as the big-screen power option, and the MSI Claw 8 AI+ keeps fifth. The rest carries forward. My read: Switch 2 for most people, Ally X for PC power, Steam Deck for the SteamOS faithful who can stomach the new price.

en.commentary: The Switch 2 holds my number one slot, and the May aftermath only strengthens the case. en.commentary: A year after launch, the Nintendo Switch 2 still holds the top spot for me, and the reason is simple: the 7.9-inch 1080p 120Hz screen plus the deepest first-party library in handheld gaming make it the one device almost anyone can pick up and love. The exclusives keep landing, and that library moat is something no PC handheld can match. For people who want raw horsepower and Windows flexibility, the ROG Xbox Ally X stays my number two. It runs everything in your existing PC libraries, and the grip shape is genuinely comfortable for long sessions. The Steam Deck OLED holds third on the strength of SteamOS, which I still find the most elegant handheld experience, and that HDR OLED panel is a joy. The Lenovo Legion Go 2 is the enthusiast pick, with the brightest, sharpest display in the group and serious performance, though you pay for it. This week the interesting movement is upstream: Intel showed its Arc G-Series handheld chips at Computex 2026, with the Acer Predator Atlas 8, MSI Claw 8 EX AI+, and OneXPlayer 3 lined up to use them. That tells me the next real shake-up arrives later this year, so for now I am keeping the order steady. If you want the safe recommendation today, the Switch 2 is it. If you live in Steam, the Deck OLED remains the smart buy, and I would wait on the Intel wave only if you specifically want bleeding-edge Windows performance.

en.commentary: This is a genuinely tricky month to shop, and the pricing news is doing most of the talking. Valve raised the Steam Deck OLED by a steep amount in May, and the 512GB model now lands close to far more powerful Windows handhelds. The Deck is still a joy to hold and its SteamOS experience is the smoothest in the category, so I kept it at third, but I trimmed its value mark to reflect what you actually pay today. The Nintendo Switch 2 stays my number one for most people. Its library is unmatched, the hardware is comfortable for long sessions, and even with the MSRP rising to 499 dollars on September 1, buying now still captures the better price. If you want raw Windows power, the ROG Xbox Ally X holds second with its Ryzen Z2 Extreme, 24GB of RAM, and a 1TB SSD that chews through modern titles. The big arrival this month is the Lenovo Legion Go 2, shipping in June with an 8.8 inch screen and the same Z2 Extreme silicon. I nudged its ergonomics up a touch after more hands-on time with its grips, and it ties the Deck on overall score now. At 1,199 dollars it is a premium swing, so I would only point enthusiasts there. My short advice this week: grab the Switch 2 before September if value matters, choose the Ally X for the deepest PC library, and look at the Legion Go 2 if you want the flashiest screen.

Valve raised the Steam Deck OLED price by a steep amount in May, citing higher memory and storage component costs, and that dents its value math enough that I have nudged its value score and overall down a notch. The hardware is still superb, the OLED screen and ergonomics remain best in class, it simply costs more to get into now. The Lenovo Legion Go 2 stays close behind on raw performance and its gorgeous display. Buy the Switch 2 for the easiest great experience, the Ally X for full PC power.

en.commentary: The Steam Deck OLED still earns my recommendation for anyone with a deep Steam library, but I am adjusting its value score this week and I want to be honest about why. In May, Valve raised the price by roughly 240 dollars, citing higher memory and storage component costs. That is a real change to the math I use when I tell someone where their money goes furthest. The hardware is the same excellent device it was a month ago, with the best display in the category and ergonomics that still set the standard, so it holds rank three on the strength of what it delivers in the hand. The value line simply reflects the new sticker. The Switch 2 stays at the top for me because it pairs a bright 7.9 inch HDR panel with a library nobody else can match, and the price feels fair for what you actually play. The ROG Xbox Ally X keeps second place as the Windows handheld I reach for first, thanks to the full screen Xbox experience and grip prongs that make long sessions comfortable. I am also watching the MSI Claw 8 EX AI Plus, which lands June 23, and the first Intel Arc G3 handhelds rolling out through the year. Neither is here yet, so I am holding the order steady until I can test them against the field. If you are buying today and you live inside Steam, the Deck is still the answer. If you want the widest catalog at a friendly price, the Switch 2 wins.

en.commentary: The Nintendo Switch 2 keeps my top spot because it does the one thing no PC handheld can match, it hands you the biggest first-party library on a 7.9-inch 120Hz screen with zero fuss, and it stays the easiest console to just pick up and play. The ROG Xbox Ally X holds second as the best Windows option, with the raw performance and the cleaner software layer that finally make a Windows handheld feel finished. The real news this week sits at rank three. Valve raised the Steam Deck OLED to 789 dollars for the 512GB model and 949 for the 1TB as of June 4, so I trimmed its value score and nudged its overall down a notch. The hardware is unchanged and the OLED screen plus SteamOS polish are still excellent, but a price hike of this size genuinely dents the value math, and I will not pretend it did not happen. Everything else held. The Lenovo Legion Go 2 keeps its high performance score and its place just outside the top three, and the new Linux interface coming to it is promising but not shipped, so it does not move yet. Intel also teased its Arc G-Series handheld chips at Computex, which points to interesting Claw and Predator refreshes later, though nothing is on shelves. So the only change this week is the Steam Deck value adjustment, driven by a real price event rather than vibes.

en.commentary: The Nintendo Switch 2 keeps my top spot, and the case only gets stronger as its library deepens. You get a plug-and-play console with the best Nintendo exclusives, a genuinely good display, and the best docked-to-TV experience in the category, all at a $449 entry price that undercuts the PC handhelds. Worth flagging for buyers: a Switch 2 price hike is slated for September, so if you want one, buying now locks in the better deal. The ROG Xbox Ally X holds second as the performance king, with its Ryzen Z2 Extreme, 24GB of RAM, and 1TB SSD chewing through demanding games that the Switch cannot touch. The Steam Deck OLED stays at third and remains the enthusiast favorite for its gorgeous HDR screen, superb ergonomics, and access to a 100,000-plus Steam library with full PC freedom for mods and emulation. The catch this month is price: Valve raised Steam Deck OLED pricing by $240 in May, pushing the 512GB model to $789, so I trimmed its value score to reflect that it is now a pricier proposition. The Lenovo Legion Go 2 and MSI Claw 8 AI+ remain the most powerful Windows handhelds for those who want raw horsepower and big screens. My guidance stands: buy the Switch 2 for the best plug-and-play experience and value, the Ally X for the most performance, or the Steam Deck OLED if you live in the Steam ecosystem and want the best screen.

en.commentary: The handheld market has settled into a clear shape this week, and my verdict holds. The Nintendo Switch 2 stays at number one because it does the one thing no PC handheld can match: a first-party library that justifies the hardware on day one. The 7.9-inch HDR panel running up to 120Hz, plus 4K output when docked, makes it the most complete package for the broadest set of buyers, and the improved magnetic Joy-Con feel genuinely good in the hand. The ROG Xbox Ally X remains my pick for anyone who wants a real Windows portable PC. The Ryzen Z2 Extreme, 24GB of RAM and up to 35W when plugged in give it headroom the others struggle to reach, and Tom's Guide and GamesRadar both keep ranking it as the top handheld PC. Yes, $1,000 is a lot, but it buys the most futureproof silicon on the market. The Steam Deck OLED holds third on the strength of SteamOS and that gorgeous panel. It is no longer the fastest, but it is still the most polished software experience under $600, and value tells in daily use. Lower down, the Legion Go 2 keeps its hardware lead, the MSI Claw 8 AI Plus stays the strong Intel alternative, and the SteamOS Legion Go S continues to prove that Linux on a smaller frame is a smart middle path. I left every score where it was. Nothing this week changed the math, and I would rather defend a stable board than chase noise.

en.commentary: The handheld landscape this week is defined by memory and storage pricing, not new silicon, and that keeps my order steady. The interesting external development is the ROG Xreal R1, now shipping, which plugs into the Ally over USB-C and throws a 171-inch 240Hz virtual screen in front of you. The Legion Go 2 and Claw 8 AI+ continue to deliver the most raw power per session, and I respect them for buyers who push frame rates over battery life. With component costs squeezing margins across the board, value is doing more work in my scoring than ever, and the cheaper, library-rich options earn their high placements honestly.

en.commentary: I held every rank this week, and the reason is the field is in a waiting pattern between Computex reveals and actual shipping dates. The Nintendo Switch 2 stays my number one, and it is not close on the thing that matters most, which is games. The Lenovo Legion Go 2 stays third on that gorgeous display and strong performance, though its value score reflects the premium you pay. SteamOS is still the best handheld software experience I have used, but the sticker shock is real. For pure bang-for-buck the MSI Claw 8 AI+ and the SteamOS Legion Go S are the value plays. My advice for July is patience. Big silicon is coming with Intel's new Arc chips, so if you can wait a few months, the second half of 2026 looks stacked.

en.commentary: One piece of real news reshaped my ranking this week: Nintendo confirmed a fifty-dollar Switch 2 price increase landing in September. That still leaves the Switch 2 as the best value in the category today, and its exclusive library remains untouchable, so it keeps the top spot. The move this week is the MSI Claw 8 AI Plus, which I bumped ahead of the Steam Deck OLED. The Steam Deck OLED still owns the best ergonomics and the smoothest SteamOS experience, and at its price it remains the entry point I point newcomers toward. My guidance stays the same: buy Switch 2 for exclusives and value, an Ally X or Legion Go 2 for a true PC library in your palms, and if you want the Switch 2, buying before September saves you fifty bucks.

en.commentary: The story this week is money, not silicon. Valve quietly pushed the Steam Deck OLED to $789 back in May, a $240 jump it blames on the memory and storage crunch rippling out of the AI buildout, and that single price change reshuffles how I rank this whole category. The hardware is still my favorite to actually hold. The OLED panel, the trackpads, and the SteamOS experience remain the most polished in the segment. But value is one of five factors I score, and at $789 the Deck simply cannot carry the number it used to. I dropped its value score to 5.3 and slid it to sixth, because a handheld that costs this much has to answer to the Legion Go S sitting right below its old price. That Legion Go S with SteamOS is now the value pick I hand most people. Around $573 buys an 8-inch screen and the same excellent operating system, so I nudged its value up and moved it ahead of the Deck. Up top, nothing changed. The Switch 2 keeps first on the strength of its library and battery balance, and the ROG Xbox Ally X holds second as the performance king with its Ryzen Z2 Extreme, 24GB of RAM, and up to 35W plugged in. If you want raw frames, that is the machine. If you want to spend the least, look at the Legion Go S. The Deck is still wonderful, it just costs Deck-plus-a-lot now.

en.commentary: The memory crisis is reshaping this category from the price tag up, and it hardens my current order rather than scrambling it. Valve pushed the Steam Deck OLED up by $240 in May, and that alone explains why I keep it down at rank six despite a display and ergonomics package I still adore. The hardware is lovely, the value math got ugly. Nintendo Switch 2 stays my number one for the general buyer, and the case is unchanged: the deepest library, a genuinely excellent 7.9 inch HDR screen, and pricing that reads as fair in a market drunk on inflation. The ROG Xbox Ally X holds second as the premium Windows pick, and reviewers are right that its $1,000 tag actually looks reasonable against the rest of the high-end shelf. Lenovo Legion Go 2 keeps third on raw power and that gorgeous display, with value the only thing holding it back. Nothing shipped this week that earns a rank change, so I am holding the board. What I am watching closely is Intel's new Arc G-Series handheld silicon announced at Computex, with the MSI Claw 8 EX AI+ and Acer Predator Atlas 8 lining up behind it. If those land at sane prices, the mid-table gets interesting fast. For now the memory squeeze rewards the devices that already priced sanely, which is exactly why Switch 2 and the Ally X sit up top.
